title: Rewrite security-checklist.md for Clojure/JVM + CLJS
description: Current checklist covers Astro/Vercel: PUBLIC_ env vars, Mux signing, Vercel headers, npm audit. Rewrite for this stack: JVM secrets/env, SSRF on the configurable ingest URL, unsafe read-string/EDN, Reagent XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML, CORS on the SSE endpoint, MapLibre tile-provider key handling, and Clojure dependency auditing.
close_reason: Closed

title: Rewrite validation-boundaries.md around the ingest boundary
description: Current doc says the stack has essentially no trust boundaries (static site, no forms, no API). That is now false: ultrafeeder's aircraft.json is the primary untrusted input, plus the HTTP API and SSE stream. Build the doc around Malli schemas in the shared .cljc domain validating at ingest.
close_reason: Closed

title: Internet-exposure hardening: TLS, headers, SSE limits
description: The app is internet-facing, which is beyond what docs currently assume. Reverse proxy with TLS in front of http-kit, security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options), a cap on concurrent SSE connections and per-IP limits so anonymous browsers cannot exhaust the server, and an update to security-checklist.md and validation-boundaries.md to reflect the new exposure in the same change.
acceptance_criteria: TLS terminates at the reverse proxy; CSP, HSTS, and X-Content-Type-Options verified on responses; a concurrent-SSE cap and per-IP limit are enforced and tested; security-checklist.md and validation-boundaries.md are updated in the same change.
notes: PRIVACY (Overseer 2026-07-11): treat the receiver position as a server-side secret - record it as such in validation-boundaries.md / security-checklist.md when updating them for internet exposure. Also ensure /data endpoints of the feeder itself are not proxied through.
close_reason: TLS terminates at a Caddy sidecar (only published ports; app port unpublished); CSP/HSTS/nosniff/Referrer-Policy/Permissions-Policy verified on real responses through the proxy and the CSP verified violation-free in headless Chromium against the release build; concurrent-SSE cap (ADSB_SSE_MAX_CLIENTS=100) and per-IP cap (ADSB_SSE_MAX_PER_IP=4) enforced atomically in the stream registry with 503+Retry-After, proven with live curl connections at cap=2 incl. slot-freeing; XFF trusted only behind the proxy (rightmost entry) after finding http-kit poisons :remote-addr with leftmost XFF; receiver-position-as-secret and never-proxy-the-feeder recorded in security-checklist.md + validation-boundaries.md in the same commit (f843178). bb test + bb lint clean.

close_reason: Promoted emergency? into adsb.aircraft (squawk->emergency-kind -> :hijack/:radio-failure/:general; emergency-kind; emergency?); geo delegates its unchanged boolean feature property. Added :aircraft/emergencies sub and adsb.ui.alert ribbon (role=alert, under header, names callsign/squawk/meaning, click selects, stacks multiples, clears when calm). (7500/7600/7700 + kinds + 0000/absent), alert_test (appears/names/click/clears/multiple), panel+sidebar badge meaning. bb test:clj 403 assertions, bb test:cljs 420 assertions, bb lint all clean. description: The pure shared geo module the README promises: bearing, distance, bounds math, and conversion of the aircraft picture into a GeoJSON FeatureCollection with the properties the map styling needs (track, altitude, emergency, stale). Pure cljc, no map dependency, fully unit-tested against the fixture cast.

title: Imperative aircraft layer via GeoJSON setData
description: The architectural centerpiece: a MapLibre GeoJSON source updated via setData on every SSE tick, entirely outside React. Hundreds of aircraft at 1 Hz must not touch the Reagent render path. re-frame owns the chrome; the map owns the planes. Includes the interop seam that lets tests fake the map object per testing-setup.md.
acceptance_criteria: Aircraft updates flow through setData with zero Reagent re-renders (asserted in a test via render counting); the fake-map seam lets CLJS tests capture setData payloads; hundreds of aircraft at 1 Hz stay smooth.
close_reason: Imperative aircraft layer landed, commit c7f2672. adsb.map.aircraft-layer: attach! waits on the map's load event, adds the GeoJSON source + a NEUTRAL circle layer through the grown maplibre seam (on-load!/add-source!/add-layer!/set-source-data! — clj->js at the very edge, so tests capture Clojure data), then starts reagent.core/track! over the :aircraft/picture subscription OUTSIDE any component: one picture change = one geo/aircraft-picture->feature-collection + one setData, zero React. Zero-re-render PROOF in adsb.map.aircraft-layer-test: shell mounted for real, 25 :stream/received frames dispatched — 25 setData calls (+1 load flush), map component render count pinned at 1, mount count 1, and unmount stops pushes. Ordering hazard: frames arriving before load land in app-db (wholesale-replaced per adsb-2yu.2), so app-db IS the latest-wins buffer; load flushes the current picture exactly once — tested (2 early frames -> 1 push, latest only). Clock: stale judged at js/Date.now at push time (frame arrival) via redef-able now-ms — the imperative edge owns the clock, the domain takes it as an argument. Feature properties icao/callsign/track/altitude(incl "ground")/emergency/stale all proven to reach the source, so adsb-2yu.5 is pure style-expression work on layer-spec. Browser suite 52/265, JVM 94/370, lint clean. NOTE: bb test:cljs currently fails to connect to the long-running shadow-cljs watch (SERVER INSTANCE OUT OF DATE after a concurrent deps.edn change) — suite was run via 'npx shadow-cljs compile test --force-spawn' + the Playwright driver; the dev watch needs a restart to pick up new deps, left untouched.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
APP-DB CONTRACT from adsb-2yu.2 (SSE client), the read surface for the imperative map layer:

:aircraft/picture — icao(string) -> domain aircraft (namespaced :aircraft/* keys, decoded via adsb.wire/wire->picture). Subscribe with (rf/subscribe [:aircraft/picture]); {} before the first frame. Every snapshot/update frame WHOLESALE-REPLACES this key (never a merge), so this ratom is the full current sky each tick — an aircraft absent from the newest frame is simply gone. Read it OUTSIDE React: add-watch the reaction (or a re-frame/subscribe deref inside a reagent.core/track + add-on-dispose, or an fx on :stream/received) and push straight into the MapLibre GeoJSON source via setData. Do NOT mount a component per aircraft. Aircraft values may lack :aircraft/position (never-positioned targets) — those produce no map feature (adsb.geo handles the projection; GeoJSON is [lon lat]).

:stream/connection — :live | :reconnecting | :down, via (rf/subscribe [:stream/connection]). For the header indicator (adsb-dgb.3), not the map layer.

Note: the live EventSource is NOT in app-db (it is a stateful JS object held in a private atom in adsb.stream). The map layer needs only the two subs above.

title: SSE client with reconnect and connection state
description: EventSource wrapper: parse snapshot and update events into app-db aircraft state, reconnect with backoff when the stream drops, and expose connection status (live / reconnecting / down) as a re-frame sub so the chrome can show it honestly. The map should recover from a server restart without a page reload.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot and update events land in app-db; killing the server flips the connection sub to reconnecting and recovery is automatic without a page reload; reconnect attempts back off.
close_reason: SSE client delivered. adsb.stream.source: EventSource seam (Connection protocol + connect!, redef-able like adsb.map.maplibre / adsb.ingest.source). adsb.stream: re-frame client — decodes each snapshot/update frame with the shared adsb.wire codec and WHOLESALE-REPLACES :aircraft/picture (icao -> namespaced domain aircraft; never a merge), and drives :stream/connection honestly: :reconnecting while EventSource auto-retries a CONNECTING drop, own exponential backoff (1s->30s cap, reset on open) when the source reaches CLOSED, :down surfaced past 3 attempts while still retrying — so a server restart heals with no page reload. Live EventSource lives in a private atom, not app-db; app-db holds only :aircraft/picture and :stream/connection (subs of the same names). Wired into adsb.core/init!. app-db contract for the map layer documented on adsb-2yu.4. Browser tests fake the seam (snapshot populates picture, update replaces wholesale, :live->:reconnecting->:down->:live lifecycle, backoff grows) under day8.re-frame.test isolation. bb test:cljs 45/228, bb test:clj 83/341, bb lint clean. Commit 2318c22. NOTE: src/cljc/adsb/wire.cljc shows an uncommitted reformat from a concurrent agent — left untouched, not in this commit.

title: SSE fan-out of aircraft state
description: The stream the browser lives on: on connect, send a full snapshot of the current picture, then push updates at ~1 Hz. Defines the wire format (JSON from the domain model - decide and document how namespaced keys serialize). Client registry with cleanup on disconnect, and a policy for slow consumers so one stalled browser cannot back-pressure the loop or leak channels.
acceptance_criteria: A new client receives a full snapshot then ~1 Hz updates; disconnect removes the client without leaking channels; a slow-consumer policy is implemented and documented; periodic SSE heartbeat comments keep the stream alive through buffering proxies; the wire format is documented.
notes: PRIVACY (Overseer 2026-07-11): receiver position is private. The wire format must exclude the receiver position AND all receiver-relative fields (r_dst, r_dir) - they are trivially invertible to the antenna location. Add a test asserting these fields never appear in SSE payloads.
close_reason: Delivered the SSE stream + boot integration. GET /api/stream: snapshot-then-updates at ~1 Hz over http-kit async channels, ': hb' heartbeats every 15s, client registry with on-close removal, drop-and-close slow-consumer policy (full-picture frames bound the backlog). Wire format is adsb.wire (cljc, bidirectional codec, allowlist projection): flat unqualified kebab-case JSON, envelope {at, aircraft[...]}; receiver position / r_dst / r_dir / rssi can never reach the wire by construction - privacy tests over the projection AND over serialized frames from the real fixture pipeline. adsb.main/start! is the one composition root (validate feeder URL loudly, resolve receiver position once, range-gate in the poll callback per nqf.3 handoff, age-out folded into the broadcast tick, live feeder status on /healthz); :dev alias boots adsb.main so bb dev streams. Required http-kit 2.9.0-beta1 (2.8.x never detects HTTP-streaming disconnects - own commit 0d3c7f8). bb test (83 clj tests/341 assertions + 41 cljs/210) and bb lint clean. Feature commit b0b8304, scoped pathspecs only. See bead comments for the full wire spec and decisions.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text: WIRE FORMAT: see adsb.wire ns docstring (src/cljc/adsb/wire.cljc).

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
WIRE FORMAT (the contract adsb-2yu.2 builds against; canonical doc: adsb.wire ns docstring, src/cljc/adsb/wire.cljc):
- Endpoint: GET /api/stream, Content-Type text/event-stream. On connect one 'snapshot' event, then 'update' events at ~1 Hz, ': hb' heartbeat comment every 15 s. Frames carry increasing 'id:' lines; Last-Event-ID is ignored on reconnect - every frame is the FULL picture, so a reconnect just takes a fresh snapshot.
- Event names: snapshot, update. Both carry the same JSON envelope: {"at": <epoch-ms>, "aircraft": [<wire aircraft>...]}. Updates are NOT deltas; treat every frame as a wholesale replacement.
- Wire aircraft: flat UNqualified kebab-case keys (same choice as geo.cljc feature properties), absent facts omitted, never defaulted: icao, callsign, lat, lon, altitude (ft), on-ground (true only), squawk, ground-speed (kt), track (deg), baro-rate (fpm), seen-at (epoch-ms last heard), position-suspect (true only - the nqf.3 spoofing flag, surfaced).
- The codec is cljc and bidirectional: the frontend should decode with adsb.wire/wire->picture (or wire->aircraft) so the contract cannot drift.
PRIVACY: aircraft->wire is an ALLOWLIST projection - receiver position, r_dst/r_dir, and any receiver-relative key can never reach the wire by construction. rssi is also withheld (it measures the receiver, not the aircraft) - if the sidebar ever wants signal strength, that is a deliberate decision for a new bead. Enforced by adsb.wire-test (both platforms, incl. a poisoned-aircraft test) and adsb.stream.broadcast-test/wire-privacy over real serialized HTTP frames from the fixture pipeline.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
SLOW-CONSUMER POLICY (documented in adsb.stream.broadcast ns docstring): DROP-AND-CLOSE. http-kit async channels do not backpressure - send! only enqueues and its boolean says whether the channel was still open - so any client whose send! returns false is closed and unregistered on the spot; on-close removes clients whose sockets die. What bounds a stalled-but-open consumer: every frame is the full picture, never a queued backlog of deltas, so the server buffers at most one bounded frame per tick until the OS gives up on the socket and send! fails. A dropped client reconnects and gets a fresh snapshot.

DEPENDENCY NOTE: http-kit bumped 2.8.0 -> 2.9.0-beta1 in its own commit (0d3c7f8). On 2.8.x, plain-HTTP-streaming close detection is broken (closeKey notifies the accept-time AsyncChannel, not the per-request one - #375 regression fixed by #578), so :on-close never fired and send! never failed: SSE clients would leak forever. Verified empirically both ways; the disconnect-cleanup test fails on 2.8.x, passes on 2.9.0-beta1. Watch for a stable 2.9.0 and bump when it lands.

OTHER DECISIONS:
- Age-out cadence folded into the broadcast tick: the broadcaster's injected picture fn is adsb.state/age-out!, which prunes AND returns the picture - one scheduler for both jobs, runs even with zero clients.
- One composition path: adsb.main/start! is the only wiring (poll -> range gate -> state -> SSE/HTTP); the :dev alias now boots adsb.main, so bb dev streams too and REQUIRES ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL (fails loudly, Boundary 3). adsb.http.server/-main removed.
- nqf.3 handoff wired: receiver/resolve-position! once at boot, plausibility/gate-range in the poll callback before state/apply-batch!; the receiver position lives only in the composition closure - never state, never wire, never logged.
- healthz: feeder-status now reports the live poller status (starting/ok/down) via an injected status fn; 'unknown' only when no poller is wired (bare handler in tests).

title: HTTP server: reitit routes, Malli coercion, static assets
description: http-kit server with reitit routing: /api/aircraft/:icao with Malli-coerced path params and schema-checked responses per validation-boundaries.md Boundary 2, a health endpoint for the container orchestrator, static serving of the compiled frontend, and a start/stop lifecycle that is REPL-friendly. No hand-rolled parsing in handlers; no keyword interning of user input.
acceptance_criteria: A malformed :icao is rejected by Malli coercion with a 400 before any handler code runs; responses are schema-checked; the health endpoint reports feeder reachability; the compiled frontend is served; start/stop is idempotent from the REPL.
close_reason: HTTP server implemented under src/clj/adsb/http/: reitit router with Malli coercion on /api/aircraft/:icao (garbage icao -> 400 in middleware, no keyword interning), schema-checked responses, /healthz with stubbed feeder-status, static serving of resources/public, and idempotent start!/stop! now booted by bb dev via the :dev alias. aircraft-detail reads an injected state-lookup defaulted to empty state (404s until adsb-nqf.2). bb test:clj and bb lint clean. Commit 3f1c071.

title: Aircraft state store + staleness domain
description: The current picture of the sky. Pure cljc domain fns in adsb.aircraft: merge an incoming batch into the picture keyed by ICAO hex, stale? by seen-age with time passed as an argument (never read the clock in cljc), and age-out removal of long-silent aircraft. Backend holds the picture in one atom updated by the poll loop. The long-silent and never-positioned cast members are the tests.
acceptance_criteria: Pure cljc: merging a batch updates the picture keyed by hex; stale? and age-out take time as an argument, never reading a clock; the long-silent cast member ages out and never-positioned is retained without a position; the backend atom is updated only by the poll loop.
close_reason: Pure merge-batch/age-out/stale?/positioned? in adsb.aircraft (seen-at-ms = capture - seen; position retained across position-less updates, other absent fields go absent; retained across missed polls, aged out at 300s silence). Thin atom shell adsb.state wired as the http server's default :state-lookup. cljc+clj tests against the cast; bb test and bb lint clean. Commit b496548.

title: Source protocol + ultrafeeder polling source
description: adsb.ingest.source/Source protocol and its first implementation: poll /data/aircraft.json at ~1 Hz. ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL comes from the environment only, is validated at boot (must parse, must be http/https, fail loudly at startup - see validation-boundaries.md Boundary 3). The loop backs off when the feeder is unreachable and never dies; a feeder outage is a status, not a crash.
acceptance_criteria: Boot fails loudly on a missing, malformed, or non-http(s) ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL; the poll loop survives a feeder outage with visible backoff and recovers when the feeder returns; the Source protocol has a second trivial implementation (test stub) proving the seam; ingest tests replay the recorded fixture, never a live feeder.
notes: Real feeder: http://dietpi.local:8100 (ultrafeeder on dietpi.local). SBS on 30003 and Beast on 30005, same host, for the future Source swap.
close_reason: Delivered adsb.ingest.source (Source protocol open!/fetch!/close! + fn-source stub), adsb.ingest.config (pure validate-feeder-url, fails loudly naming ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL), adsb.ingest.ultrafeeder (http-kit + cheshire Source feeding ->aircraft-batch), and adsb.ingest.poll (~1 Hz loop, log-once-per-state-change, exponential backoff to 30s cap, never dies, feeder status via poll/status). bb test:clj green (52 tests / 216 assertions), bb lint clean. Committed ae678d1 (only my 8 files, pathspec). RESIDUAL: poller is not wired at boot — main.clj is owned by adsb-kh4.1 and untouched here; healthz (adsb-kbm.2) + state store/SSE (adsb-nqf.2) must construct the poller, hold it, and read poll/status. See bead comment for the exact status-atom shape and callback seam.

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text: Feeder status lives in the poller returned by adsb.ingest.poll/start! — read it with (adsb.ingest.poll/status poller), which derefs an atom holding {:feeder/status :starting|:ok|:down, :feeder/last-success-ms, :feeder/last-error}. For adsb-kbm.2 (/healthz) and adsb-nqf.2 (state store + SSE chrome): the poller is not yet constructed anywhere (main.clj is owned by adsb-kh4.1, untouched here). Whoever wires start! at boot should hold the returned poller and expose poll/status to the healthz handler and SSE. The on-batch! callback is the seam to the state store (adsb-nqf.2).

title: Frontend map core
description: The browser half: a MapLibre map that renders live aircraft pushed over SSE. The load-bearing rule from AUTEUR and the README applies throughout - the aircraft layer bypasses React entirely and is fed via GeoJSON setData; re-frame owns only the chrome.
acceptance_criteria: Open the app and watch live aircraft move, styled by altitude and heading, aging out honestly - with the 1 Hz path never touching React.
close_reason: All six children closed: app shell, SSE client, geo/GeoJSON, imperative setData layer (zero-re-render proven), data-driven styling, continuous staleness fade. Live aircraft render and age honestly in bb dev; the 1 Hz path never touches React.

title: Streaming and HTTP API
description: The seam between backend and browser: an http-kit + reitit server with Malli-coerced routes, and the SSE fan-out that pushes the aircraft picture to every connected browser at ~1 Hz. Boundary 2 of docs/validation-boundaries.md lives here.
acceptance_criteria: A browser connects and receives the live picture over SSE; every HTTP surface is Malli-coerced at the edge and schema-checked on the way out.
close_reason: Both children closed: reitit HTTP server with Malli coercion, SSE fan-out with privacy-allowlisted wire format + boot integration. Verified streaming real aircraft end-to-end.

title: Ingest pipeline and live aircraft state
description: The backend half of the data flow, downstream of the validation boundary built in adsb-bvi.3: poll the ultrafeeder behind the Source protocol, keep the poll loop alive through feeder outages, and maintain the current picture of the sky - a state store keyed by ICAO hex that ages out silent aircraft. Ends where the SSE stream begins.
acceptance_criteria: A running backend maintains an accurate current picture of the sky from a real feeder, survives outages and malformed entries, and flags implausible data instead of trusting or hiding it.
close_reason: All five children closed: Source protocol + poller, state store + staleness, plausibility (range gate + jump flagging), replay Source, MLAT confidence. Backend ingest verified live against the real feeder and feederless via replay.

title: Malli schemas + ingest boundary
description: src/cljc/adsb/schema.cljc and the ingest path per docs/validation-boundaries.md: coerce alt_baro (number OR the string 'ground'), keep absent fields absent rather than defaulting to 0, trim space-padded callsigns, drop never-positioned aircraft, enforce plausibility limits separately from schema validity, and guarantee one malformed aircraft cannot kill the batch or the poll loop.
design: Never-positioned reconciliation (bead text said 'drop never-positioned', adsb-nqf.2 expects them retained): DROP only identity-less entries (missing/malformed hex — cannot be tracked, deduped, or aged) and schema-invalid entries (one bounded log line each); KEEP position-less aircraft as domain aircraft without :aircraft/position (12/51 of the real capture, ~24% — they belong in the sidebar/counts, and simply get no map feature). docs/validation-boundaries.md and the testing-standards cast row updated to match. Plausibility is a separate layer: implausible altitude/gs drops the FIELD, keeps the aircraft, never clamps (bounds are number-based, not :int — real gs is a double). Receiver-range gating and jump detection deliberately NOT built (adsb-nqf.3). Also consolidated per orchestrator: schema/icao-address (anchored + case-insensitive; the routes.clj placeholder regex was unanchored) now backs raw :hex, domain :aircraft/icao, and the :icao path param; aircraft-detail 200 body is schema/aircraft.
acceptance_criteria: alt_baro accepts a number or the string ground; absent fields stay absent - no zero defaults; callsigns are trimmed; never-positioned aircraft are dropped at the boundary; plausibility limits are enforced separately from schema validity; a batch with one malformed entry yields the rest plus one log line, and the poll loop survives.
notes: From the real capture (adsb-bvi.2): alt_baro is ABSENT on ~13% of observations (bare mode_s targets carry only hex/messages/rssi/seen) - the schema must treat alt_baro as optional, not just number-or-ground. Fixture: test/resources/aircraft-sample.json (51 aircraft, warts preserved byte-exact).
close_reason: Trust boundary landed in 734e46e. schema.cljc: raw-aircraft honoring reality (alt_baro number|"ground"|absent, nullable optionals, absent-stays-absent), domain aircraft schema, consolidated anchored case-insensitive icao-address (also fixes the unanchored routes.clj placeholder regex), and number-based plausibility limits as a separate layer. ingest/coerce.cljc: ->aircraft (keeps position-less aircraft, drops identity-less; trims callsigns, lower-cases hex, drops implausible FIELDS without clamping) and unkillable ->aircraft-batch (one bounded log line per reject, clj/cljs catch backstop). Comprehensive cljc tests incl. batch resilience with mangled/non-map entries, log-count + log-boundedness tests, and a whole-real-fixture test: 51/51 accepted, 39 positioned / 12 never-positioned retained, all domain-schema-valid. bb test:clj 103 assertions green, bb lint clean, browser suite 87 assertions green in headless Chromium. Never-positioned decision recorded on the bead and written into validation-boundaries.md + testing-standards cast.

title: Record a real aircraft.json payload as a test fixture
description: Capture a genuine payload from the ultrafeeder and commit it to test/resources/aircraft-sample.json — warts included: the 'ground' altitude strings, the aircraft with no lat/lon, the space-padded callsigns, the nulls. Hand-written fixtures are a fiction of what we THINK the feeder sends; a recording is what it does send. Everything in docs/validation-boundaries.md is asserted from knowledge of readsb/tar1090's format, NOT from looking at this specific feeder's output — so verify each claimed quirk against the real capture and correct the doc if any is wrong.
acceptance_criteria: A committed sample payload; each quirk listed in the validation-boundaries.md table either confirmed present or the doc corrected.
notes:
Feeder confirmed live at http://dietpi.local:8100/data/aircraft.json (2026-07-11).

FIXTURE CAPTURED 2026-07-11: 10 polls at ~25s intervals, 49-52 aircraft each (348+ aircraft-observations). Richest capture (poll 10, 51 aircraft) committed as test/resources/aircraft-sample.json. PRIVACY REDACTION: r_dst and r_dir stripped from every aircraft (39 of 51 had them) via byte-preserving text surgery; verified JSON-equal to original minus exactly those keys; no receiver lat/lon fields existed in the payload. Provenance documented in test/resources/README.md.

TRAP TABLE VERIFICATION (against all 10 captures):
- alt_baro number: CONFIRMED (abc0e4 alt_baro=34775). Only int seen; no floats.
- alt_baro string 'ground': NOT OBSERVED in capture window (no airport-surface traffic in range). Claim stands, uncontradicted.
- lat/lon absent entirely: CONFIRMED (a10202, abe075, ad072c in fixture; 102 occurrences total).
- flight space-padded to 8 chars: CONFIRMED (ac5697 'SWA349  ', a5d4c3 'GTI9071 '; 397 occurrences). Also flight absent: CONFIRMED (abc0e4, adc9b6).
- hex ~-prefix (TIS-B/ADS-R): NOT OBSERVED. Only types seen: adsb_icao (292), mode_s (56). Uncontradicted.
- squawk string of 4 octal digits: CONFIRMED, always string, never int (a49498 '0745', a4008e '0541' — leading zeros prove type matters; 309 occurrences). '0000' itself not seen.

This is chrome, so it IS Reagent/re-frame — but it updates at feeder cadence with every aircraft in it, so watch the render cost; it may need the same escape hatch the aircraft layer uses. Open question: what happens when 40 aircraft occupy the same 2000ft? Jitter, cluster, or fan out. Related: the rejected ATC flight-progress strip rack may return as the SELECTION detail surface.
acceptance_criteria: The Stack replaces the sidebar entirely, works on phone and desktop, and a user can find and select any aircraft through it without a list ever appearing.

Vertical rate rides the same channel for free: a closing shadow reads as a descent before any number does. Must be proven at real traffic density and in both themes.

Shadow may need to become a glow, a halo, or an offset ghost — but the parallax-lift semantics must survive whatever it becomes. The aircraft layer is NOT React (see CLAUDE.md) — this lives in the MapLibre layer, so the shadow is a second symbol/circle layer driven by the same GeoJSON source, offset per-feature.
acceptance_criteria: At real feeder density, in both themes, a glance distinguishes high from low traffic without reading a single number, and a climbing aircraft is visibly climbing.

title: Load .env at the composition root (adsb.env), not the launcher
description: A JVM app cannot see .env; System/getenv reports the process environment only. Loading it in bb.edn only worked when launched via 'bb dev'. adsb.env/read! backfills the process environment from .env at the composition root, so every entry point (uberjar, bb dev, bare clojure -M:dev, IDE, REPL) boots identically. A real exported variable always beats the file, and production ships no .env, so it cannot shadow deployed config.

close_reason: adsb.env/read! backfills the process environment from .env at main's composition root. env->config was already pure and took the env map as an argument, so it was fed rather than changed. bb.edn reverted. Verified from a bare 'clojure -M' with bb absent: System/getenv nil, env/read! yields the .env value, an exported var overrides it. bb test:clj 116/460 green, bb test:cljs 479 green, bb lint clean.

title: bb.edn: load .env into the dev process environment
description: A .env file is inert for a JVM app — System/getenv only sees the exported shell env, and nothing on the classpath parses .env. Load it in bb.edn (the project's command surface) and pass it to child processes via :extra-env so 'bb dev' works from a fresh clone without a shell incantation. Real environment variables must take precedence over the file so CI/Docker overrides still win.
acceptance_criteria: bb dev picks up ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL from .env; a real exported env var overrides the file; missing .env is a no-op, not an error
close_reason: bb.edn :init parses .env and passes it to the dev task's child processes via :extra-env. Real exported env wins over the file; missing .env is a clean no-op. Verified all three cases with a temporary probe task (since bb dev's ports were occupied). Caught two EDN-reader traps along the way: bb.edn allows neither #"regex" literals nor @deref.

title: Production basemap tile source
description: The dev map can lean on a free demo style, but MapLibre demo tiles are explicitly not for production, and this app will be internet-facing. Pick a sustainable, no-token tile source - OpenFreeMap, self-hosted PMTiles/Protomaps, or similar - verify its usage policy tolerates public traffic, and wire correct attribution. If any provider requiring a key is chosen instead, that key becomes the first browser-visible secret and security-checklist.md section 3 must be updated (the docs currently promise there are none).
design:
PROVIDER DECISION: OpenFreeMap 'liberty' style — https://tiles.openfreemap.org/styles/liberty

RESEARCH / RATIONALE (evaluated 2026-07-11):
- OpenFreeMap (CHOSEN): public instance is free, no API key, no registration, no cookies. Terms (openfreemap.org) state NO limits on map views/requests, commercial use allowed, sustainable for a public hobby site (donation/sponsor funded). Zero browser-visible secret => security-checklist.md §3's 'no tokens' promise stays true, no doc change needed. Attribution 'OpenFreeMap © OpenMapTiles Data from OpenStreetMap' is carried in the style JSON and rendered automatically by MapLibre's attribution control (already enabled). 'liberty' is the neutral-rich variant (terrain/water/labels) matching the richly-rendered design lean.
- Protomaps self-hosted PMTiles: strongest for zero external dependency, but needs us to serve tiles/PMTiles ourselves — deferred as the documented fallback if OpenFreeMap's public instance ever degrades. No domain impact (only the style URL changes).
- CARTO / MapLibre demotiles: demotiles are explicitly NOT for production; CARTO basemaps have usage/attribution constraints and lean toward keyed access. Rejected.

IMPLEMENTATION: option (a) — single style URL in dev and prod (fair use permits it), replacing the DEV-ONLY demotiles URL. Renamed adsb.map.view/dev-style-url -> style-url. attributionControl stays true. No :closure-defines, no runtime config, no index.html injection needed. VARIANT (not provider) may be swapped by adsb-dgb.5 by editing the one URL.

VERIFICATION: no token/key string in built main.js (grep clean); existing map tests assert style is https + token-free (still green); manual bb dev visual check of style load + attribution.
acceptance_criteria: Production style loads from the chosen source within its usage policy; attribution is correct; either no token ships to the browser, or the docs are updated to reflect the first one.
close_reason: Production basemap set to OpenFreeMap 'liberty' (https://tiles.openfreemap.org/styles/liberty): no-token, no-key, unlimited public instance, commercial use allowed — fair use permits one URL in dev and prod, so no browser-visible secret ships and security-checklist §3 stays true (no doc change needed). Attribution 'OpenFreeMap © OpenMapTiles Data from OpenStreetMap' carried by the source TileJSON, rendered automatically by MapLibre's attribution control. Verified: release main.js grep shows the OpenFreeMap URL baked in, demotiles gone, zero token/key strings; headless-Chromium render loaded the style + 15 vector tiles, attribution control reads 'MapLibre | OpenFreeMap © OpenMapTiles Data from OpenStreetMap', Florida basemap rendered. bb test 130 tests/581 assertions green, bb lint 0/0. Commit 2a56d76. Protomaps self-hosted PMTiles documented as the fallback if the public instance degrades. Variant (not provider) may be swapped by adsb-dgb.5.

title: Replay Source: run the app and tests from the recorded fixture

Two reasons it must exist: the docs forbid testing against a live feeder, so ingest and stream tests need a deterministic Source; and bb dev should work on a laptop that cannot reach the home network. This is also the proof that the Source protocol seam actually holds.
acceptance_criteria: bb dev renders a moving map with no feeder reachable, driven by the replayed fixture; ingest and SSE tests consume the replay Source; replayed seen ages advance so staleness logic is exercised.
close_reason: Added adsb.ingest.replay: a fixture-backed Source (open!/fetch!/close!) replaying test/resources/aircraft-sample.json forever through an injected monotonic clock — positions dead-reckon along track at ground speed, seen ages accrue at half the clock so aircraft cross the 60s stale line without reaching the 300s age-out, per-lap reset keeps the picture full. Wired via ADSB_SOURCE=replay (no feeder URL required; ultrafeeder remains default and still fails loud on a missing/bad URL). Unit tests use a fake clock (advancing seen, moving positions, looping, determinism); config/main tests cover source selection. bb test:clj (93 tests, 363 assertions) and bb lint clean. Verified end-to-end: ADSB_SOURCE=replay clojure -M:dev -> /healthz feeder-status ok, SSE snapshot 39 positioned aircraft, later update 38 moved, some stale, zero false position-suspect flags. RESIDUAL: the acceptance 'bb dev renders a moving map' cannot be shown visually yet because the map aircraft layer (adsb-2yu.4) does not exist; the SSE-level proof of moving/aging aircraft is the honest equivalent today. Skipped bb test:cljs to avoid colliding with the concurrent src/cljs agent; my change is clj-only. Commit 0b403f4. Not pushed.

title: Cloud-to-home tunnel for feeder connectivity
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: the cloud backend reaches the home ultrafeeder over a VPN/tunnel (Tailscale, WireGuard, or cloudflared - pick one and document why). ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL points at the tunnel address; the feeder is never exposed publicly. Verify boot-time URL validation and the poll-loop backoff behave sanely when the tunnel is down.
design:
Architecture: Cloudflare Tunnel, service-token pattern (confirmed against current Cloudflare One docs, 2026-07).

DIRECTION: cloud backend reaches IN to the home feeder; the feeder is reachable from nowhere else. cloudflared runs on the HOME network (beside the ultrafeeder), dialing out to Cloudflare — no home inbound port, no public IP. It exposes dietpi:8100 as a private hostname (feeder.bwawan.com on the Overseer's bwawan.com zone) gated by a Cloudflare Access 'Service Auth' policy, so the hostname is NOT publicly readable without the token.

AUTH: the cloud app is a plain HTTPS client of feeder.bwawan.com. It presents a Cloudflare Access service token as CF-Access-Client-Id / CF-Access-Client-Secret headers on EVERY feeder request (aircraft.json AND receiver.json), sourced from ADSB_FEEDER_AUTH_ID / ADSB_FEEDER_AUTH_SECRET.

Alternatives rejected: (a) 'cloudflared access tcp' on the cloud side — adds a second daemon + localhost hop for no gain over an authenticated GET; (b) WARP/WireGuard — puts the whole cloud host on the home LAN, far more reach than 'read one JSON file' needs. Service token grants exactly the needed reach.

CODE: adsb.ingest.config/feeder-auth-headers builds the header map, validated both-or-neither at boot (half a credential fails loudly, like a bad URL); secret NEVER in exception message/ex-data. Threaded config -> ultrafeeder/->source -> fetch-batch! and receiver/fetch-position!.

FAILURE MODE: downed tunnel / revoked token / missing header => non-2xx from the edge => poll loop backs off + UI shows feeder unhealthy (already covered by poll backoff tests). Safe by construction.

RESIDUALS (go-live adsb-kh4.6, Overseer's Cloudflare account): create tunnel, ingress to dietpi:8100, Access app + Service Auth policy, generate token, curl verify with/without headers. No real tunnel was created here; runbook in README Deployment -> 'Cloud-to-home feeder tunnel'.
acceptance_criteria: The chosen tunnel is documented with rationale; the cloud container polls the home feeder over it; a downed tunnel behaves exactly like a feeder outage - backoff on the server, honest status in the UI; the feeder is reachable from nowhere else.
notes: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: cloudflared (Cloudflare Tunnel) from the home server. Pairs with DNS/TLS on Cloudflare if that is where the domain lands.
close_reason: Cloudflare Tunnel + Access service-token auth implemented and documented. config/feeder-auth-headers (both-or-neither boot validation, secret never logged) threaded config -> ultrafeeder + receiver fetches; compose sidecar stub replaced with home-side note + auth env placeholders; README runbook added. Tests: headers present/absent + secret-never-leaks-on-failure. bb test:clj (534) + bb test:cljs (618) + bb lint all clean. Commit a5faf7c. Operational tunnel/Access provisioning is go-live residual (adsb-kh4.6); no real tunnel created. main.clj env-threading edit left in working tree, riding with the Overseer's uncommitted dotenv work (not committed here).

close_reason: bb build now produces a deployable artifact. Added src/clj/adsb/main.clj (production -main: reads PORT [default 8280] and ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL from env — URL captured, not yet used, for adsb-nqf.1 to validate; starts adsb.http.server; blocks), build.clj + :build alias (tools.build) that AOTs adsb.main and packages resources/ incl. the shadow release frontend into target/adsb.jar, and a bb build task that runs 'shadow-cljs release app' before cutting the jar. Everything else in the acceptance criteria is met. Committed via pathspec in 1afc7cf; note deps.edn/bb.edn also carry a concurrent task's nrepl-alias edits, swept in by whole-file pathspec (preserved, not reverted).

Not pushed; concurrent-agent edits to deps.edn/main.clj/view.cljs etc. left unstaged.

title: Smooth interpolation between updates (dead reckoning)
description: Aircraft receive positions at ~1 Hz and must glide, not jump: between SSE ticks, project each positioned aircraft forward along track at ground speed via requestAnimationFrame, snapping to truth when a real update lands. Runs in the imperative map path - this must never touch React. Skip aircraft without gs/track; absent is not zero.
acceptance_criteria: Aircraft glide between ticks via rAF projection along track at ground speed and snap when a real update lands; aircraft without gs or track do not move; projection stops for stale aircraft; the map stays smooth.
close_reason: Aircraft glide between 1 Hz updates via a rAF dead-reckoning loop: pure spherical destination math + project-aircraft in adsb.geo (cljc, unit-tested against the canonical worked example), integrated in the imperative layer as a third push path off the shared !state cache — aircraft source only, trails stay real, ~20 Hz push throttle, pauses on hidden tab, stops on detach, snaps to truth on real frames, holds gs/track-less and stale aircraft. Zero React involvement proven by a rAF zero-re-render test. Full bb test (581 assertions) + lint clean. Commit aa27786.

title: Position trails with fading opacity
description: Per-aircraft position history kept client-side for the session (no persistence, per Overseer decision), rendered as a MapLibre line layer with opacity fading toward the tail. Cap history length per aircraft so memory is bounded; trails vanish when the aircraft ages out.
acceptance_criteria: A moving aircraft leaves a trail fading toward the tail; per-aircraft history is capped so memory is bounded; the trail is removed when the aircraft ages out; no React involvement.
close_reason: Fading position trails shipped: pure adsb.trails cljc (capped per-aircraft ring, append-on-change, drop-on-leave) + second GeoJSON source/line layer with line-gradient fade, wired into attach!'s track/tick path. Trails vanish with their aircraft via live-icao filtering. cljc + browser tests green; lint clean. Commit 5383292.

title: Header, legend, connection status indicator
description: The frame around the map: app header, a legend explaining the altitude ramp and emergency treatment, aircraft count, and an honest connection indicator driven by the SSE connection sub - live, reconnecting, or feeder down. When the map is stale the user must be able to tell.
acceptance_criteria: The connection indicator honestly shows live / reconnecting / down from the SSE sub; the legend matches the real altitude ramp and emergency treatment; the aircraft count updates live.
close_reason: Header (title, live total+positioned counts, UTC clock, connection chip), altitude+status legend overlay reading adsb.map.style constants directly (no drift), connection indicator honest across live/reconnecting/down. Wired into views/app-root. Browser tests green (counts render+update, three indicator states, legend swatches equal style constants); bb test + bb lint clean. Commit b6af0c7.

title: Aircraft list sidebar with sort and filters
description: A Reagent sidebar listing current aircraft with sort (altitude, distance, callsign) and simple filters (e.g. positioned-only, airborne-only). Click-to-select syncs with the map. Driven by re-frame subs over the same aircraft state the map reads - one source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: The list sorts by altitude, distance, and callsign; filters (positioned-only, airborne-only) work; click-to-select syncs with the map; list and map read the same app-db state.
close_reason: Aircraft list sidebar shipped (src/cljs/adsb/ui/sidebar.cljs + test). Reagent roster deriving from the same :aircraft/picture sub the map reads — one source of truth. Rows: callsign (icao fallback), three-state altitude (dash for absent, never 0), ground speed; badges for emergency squawk (adsb.geo/emergency?), position-suspect, MLAT. Delegated click dispatches existing [:aircraft/select icao]; selected row marked via :aircraft/selected-icao. Sorts: altitude high->low, callsign A->Z, seen-age freshest-first; absent values sort LAST in every mode (a fact the sky never reported cannot rank against ones it did). Filters: positioned-only and airborne-only (on-ground? true-or-absent semantics) as toggles; state under :ui/* keys with events+subs. ACCEPTANCE DEVIATION: distance sort dropped — it presumed a receiver reference, and the receiver position is PRIVATE by design and never reaches the browser; the only alternative (map-center distance) needs map-state plumbing outside this bead and isn't a true distance-from-antenna. Substituted seen-age sort. Full bb test (82 tests, 388 assertions) + bb lint clean. Commit ba33677 covers the two new files. Root mount in views.cljs and neutral CSS in app.css are applied on disk but left UNSTAGED: both entangle with a concurrent header/legend agent's uncommitted work; committing here would capture their WIP or reference not-yet-committed namespaces (adsb.ui.header/legend). Whoever owns views.cljs/app.css commits the mount.

title: Client-side staleness: fade and remove silent aircraft
description: Aircraft that stop transmitting must visibly age: fade as seen-age grows, disappear past the age-out threshold, using the same shared stale? domain fn as the backend so the two sides never disagree about what stale means. The long-silent cast member is the acceptance test.
acceptance_criteria: The long-silent fixture fades as it ages and disappears past the threshold; both sides use the shared stale? fn; a selected aircraft that ages out clears the selection.
notes: Scope addition from 2yu.5 residual: adsb.geo/aircraft->feature does not yet lift :aircraft/mlat? onto feature properties - add :mlat (boolean, omitted when absent) + test, so the style layer can express lower confidence. Style constants live in adsb.map.style; fade should be expression-driven off the stale/age property, constants in that ns.
close_reason: Client-side staleness shipped: geo.cljc emits continuous :age-s (+ boolean :stale kept) and filters aged-out aircraft with adsb.aircraft/aged-out?; style.cljs interpolates opacity over :age-s between domain thresholds (in seconds); :mlat lifted onto features with a smaller-icon lower-confidence cue; a 5s client tick in attach!/detach! re-pushes the cached picture with a fresh now-ms so fades progress and silent aircraft vanish between frames/stalls. cljc + browser tests cover age property, age-out filtering, mlat, and the tick (fake clock). bb test + bb lint clean. Commit b2bfbbf.

title: Aircraft selection + detail panel
description: Click an aircraft to select it: selection state in re-frame, a Reagent detail panel showing callsign, altitude, speed, track, squawk, seen-age, position - rendering absent fields as absent (a dash), never as zero. Selection survives SSE updates and clears when the aircraft ages out. Remember Boundary 4: every string in this panel came off an unauthenticated radio.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking an aircraft opens the panel; absent fields render as a dash, never zero; selection survives SSE ticks; the panel reads only re-frame subs.
close_reason: Aircraft selection state + Reagent detail panel. re-frame events :aircraft/select (hard contract w/ 2yu.5) / :aircraft/clear-selection, app-db :aircraft/selected-icao, subs :aircraft/selected-icao + derived :aircraft/selected joined against :aircraft/picture. Panel renders callsign/icao/altitude/speed/track/squawk/vrate/seen-age with absent->em-dash (never 0), suspect+mlat badges, close button. Boundary-4 XSS-proof test passes: hostile callsign renders as escaped text, no img node. Selection survives updates, closes when aircraft leaves picture; dangling icao pruned by 1Hz UI-edge clock tick. 321 cljs + 370 clj assertions green, lint clean, app build clean. Commit bd77c2e.

title: Data-driven aircraft styling: heading rotation, altitude ramp, emergency state
description: MapLibre layer style expressions - not per-feature code: icon rotated by track, color ramped by altitude, distinct emergency treatment driven by feature properties. Altitude, heading, and emergency state are the data that carry meaning (AUTEUR); make them legible at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Icons rotate with track, color follows the altitude ramp, and emergency aircraft are visually distinct - all via style expressions over feature properties, no per-feature JS; the plane icon asset is registered in the style (sprite or addImage).
close_reason: Symbol layer driven by MapLibre style expressions in new adsb.map.style ns (data). Icon: two SDF silhouettes (plane + dot for track-less) via seam add-image!, icon-rotate [get track], alignment map. Three-state altitude ramp (absent/ground/numeric) with has-guards; emergency overrides colour+size; stale fades opacity. Click contract dispatches [:aircraft/select icao] through the seam (event/UI owned by dgb.1). Tests + lint green (commit 39b7d1b).

title: Plausibility layer: range gate + position-jump flagging
description: The second validation layer from validation-boundaries.md, beyond per-field limits: drop positions beyond the receiver horizon (needs receiver position - read it from ultrafeeder receiver.json or env), drop absurd fields while keeping the aircraft, and FLAG impossible position jumps rather than dropping them - a jump is the fingerprint of spoofing and must surface, not vanish. Requires prior state, so it sits on the state store.
acceptance_criteria: A position beyond the receiver horizon drops the aircraft; an absurd altitude or speed drops the field and keeps the aircraft; an impossible position jump is flagged, not dropped, and surfaces in the data; values are never silently clamped.
notes: Live probe shows the feeder emits r_dst / r_dir (receiver-relative distance and bearing) per aircraft - useful for the range gate server-side. PRIVACY (Overseer 2026-07-11): receiver position is hidden from the public site, and r_dst/r_dir must NEVER reach the wire format - one aircraft's position plus its r_dst/r_dir locates the antenna exactly. Strip them at ingest or at serialization, and test for their absence.
close_reason: Plausibility layer built and committed (3cd83da). Range gate: adsb.ingest.plausibility/gate-range drops aircraft strictly beyond a configurable horizon (default 400 km / ~216 nm); position-less aircraft pass; nil receiver position disables the gate. Receiver position: adsb.ingest.receiver/resolve-position! once at setup - ADSB_RECEIVER_LAT/LON override wins, else feeder receiver.json (shape verified against the live feeder), else nil + one warn log; coordinates never logged. Jump flagging: >1200 kt sustained implied speed sets :aircraft/position-suspect? true - flagged never dropped/clamped; recomputed per observation (consistent follow-up clears; position-less observation inherits flag with inherited position); composed into adsb.state/apply-batch!. Schema: :aircraft/position-suspect? optional boolean. Privacy tested: coerce carries no trace of r_dst/r_dir; pipeline yields only aircraft-namespaced keys; committed receiver fixture uses synthetic coords. Docs updated (validation-boundaries.md plausibility section + receiver-position sourcing + privacy). bb test (clj 70/287 + cljs browser 37/189) and bb lint clean. Boot wiring for gate-range left as a bead comment for the SSE/integration bead (main.clj owned elsewhere).

comments.author: Brandon Correa
comments.text:
Boot wiring left for the SSE/integration bead (main.clj/deps owned elsewhere this session). Exact wiring when the poller is started:

  ;; once, at poller setup - never per poll:
  (def receiver-position
    (receiver/resolve-position! {:env (System/getenv) :base-url feeder-url}))
  ;; nil => range gate disabled, already logged once by resolve-position!

  ;; in the poll callback:
  :on-batch! (fn [batch]
               (-> batch
                   (plausibility/gate-range receiver-position
                                            plausibility/default-max-range-m)
                   (state/apply-batch! (System/currentTimeMillis))))

Namespaces: adsb.ingest.receiver (clj edge), adsb.ingest.plausibility (pure cljc). Jump flagging needs no wiring - it is already composed into adsb.state/apply-batch!. PRIVACY: never log or serialize receiver-position; env override ADSB_RECEIVER_LAT/LON wins over receiver.json.

Residual: adsb.fixtures mlat-derived comment says nqf.3 would surface MLAT confidence as a domain field - that was not in this bead's scope; needs its own bead if wanted.

title: Surface feeder status in the browser
description: The stream connection chip (dgb.3) tracks browser-to-server SSE health, but the feeder can be down while the stream is live - the UI then looks healthy while aircraft silently age out (the fade helps, but nothing says why). The backend already tracks poll/status (:ok/:down/:starting + last-success). Carry it to the client - simplest: a feeder-status field on the SSE envelope (wire.cljc + broadcast), a sub, and a second state on or beside the connection chip. Keep the two indicators semantically distinct: stream health vs feeder health.
acceptance_criteria: Killing the feeder (or tunnel) while the stream stays up visibly flips the UI to a feeder-down state within a few seconds; stream reconnect and feeder-down render distinguishably; wire envelope documented in wire.cljc docstring.
close_reason: Feeder health now rides every SSE frame: wire.cljc feeder->wire/wire->feeder allowlist (status+last-success only, error excluded), broadcast threads a :feeder thunk (main composes poll/status), client decodes to :feeder/status with a derived :feeder/health sub applying the unknowable rule (stream not :live -> :unknown), header shows a second feeder chip and relabels the stream chip's :down to 'Disconnected'. Full cljs suite (136 tests) + wire/broadcast clj tests + lint green; live-proof: real feeder -> feeder ok (36 aircraft), unreachable feeder -> feeder down while SSE stayed live, no error string on the wire. Committed 013610a.

title: Surface MLAT-derived confidence on domain aircraft
description: The mlat-derived cast member (adsb-bvi.4) exists, but coercion does not yet surface positioning confidence: a raw entry with type mlat / non-empty mlat array coerces to an ordinary positioned aircraft. Add e.g. :aircraft/mlat? (or a confidence field) at the boundary so the UI can render lower-confidence targets distinctly, update the fixture cast comment that mis-attributed this to adsb-nqf.3, and align the fixture's domain form.
acceptance_criteria: mlat-derived raw form yields a domain aircraft carrying the confidence marker; schema updated; fixture cast test asserts it; the stale comment in fixtures.cljc corrected.
close_reason: Coercion surfaces :aircraft/mlat? true for MLAT-derived positions (type=mlat OR non-empty mlat array); true-or-absent marker consistent with on-ground?/position-suspect?; schema, wire allowlist (both directions), docstrings, fixtures comment, and tests updated. bb test:clj + bb lint clean; browser suite verified via force-spawn compile + run-browser-tests.mjs (52 tests, 265 assertions, 0 failures). Commit 7965e32.

title: Add MIT LICENSE
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: the code is MIT licensed. Add the LICENSE file with the correct copyright holder and year, and reference it from the README.
acceptance_criteria: LICENSE file at repo root with MIT text and correct holder/year; README mentions the license.
close_reason: MIT LICENSE at repo root (Brandon Correa, 2026) + README license section; verified by host.

title: Receiver stats readout (no public receiver location)
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: the receiver position is private and must not be derivable from the public site - no range rings, no receiver marker, no antenna-centered default view. This bead is therefore a stats readout only: current aircraft count, positioned count, max observed range as a number, and message rate if the feeder exposes it. The backend still knows the true position for the range gate; it stays server-side.
acceptance_criteria: Stats show aircraft count, positioned count, max observed range, and message rate if available; nothing rendered or served reveals the receiver position; the map default view is a fixed general region, not the antenna.
close_reason: Numbers-only session stats readout shipped (commit 0907293): server adsb.stats computes running max range from the private receiver position and message rate from a new ultrafeeder Metadata side-channel; wire envelope gains an allowlist-projected stats map (max-range-km + message-rate scalars only, no position/counts); client adsb.ui.stats renders/dashes them via a :stats/session sub. No receiver location anywhere in the browser. bb test (117 clj + 107 cljs) and bb lint clean.

title: Aircraft enrichment from a static aircraft database
description: Look up ICAO hex in a static database (tar1090-db style) to show type, registration, and operator in the detail panel and list. Decide where the DB lives (served by backend vs bundled) and treat it as data, not truth - enrichment must degrade to absent, never block the live path.
design:
ARCHITECTURE: Browser-side enrichment against static sharded JSON served by the backend as plain files (tar1090's own pattern). NO wire-format change — the SSE wire is untouched, so the live path never waits on a lookup and the concurrent wire.cljc edit never conflicts.

DB SOURCE: tar1090-db (github.com/wiedehopf/tar1090-db), data maintained by Mictronics (mictronics.de/aircraft-database). We fetch the single stable file aircraft.csv.gz from the csv branch: https://github.com/wiedehopf/tar1090-db/raw/refs/heads/csv/aircraft.csv.gz (~8MB gz). CSV is semicolon-delimited, no header, escapechar backslash, columns: icao;registration;typecode;flags;description;year;operator; (trailing empty). We use icao(0), registration(1), typecode(2), description(4), operator(6).

LICENSE: No LICENSE file in tar1090-db (all 404) nor a stated license on the Mictronics page (online search discontinued 'due to abuse'; data compiled from public registration sources). Treat as third-party data of UNSTATED license: DATA, not truth (validation-boundaries.md), fetched at build/dev time, NEVER committed (large + license-unclear), resources/public/db/ gitignored, everything degrades to absent when the dir is missing. Documented in README.

SHARD SCHEME (ours, not tar1090's native trie): deliberate deviation. tar1090's native db/ uses an ADAPTIVE split (blocklimit 24576) with a 'children' recursion + version drift (db vs db-2); reimplementing that lazy trie walk faithfully in CLJS is more code and more fragile than a fixed prefix we own. So a build-time babashka transform (bb db:fetch) reads aircraft.csv.gz and writes resources/public/db/<first-3-hex>.json, each a JSON object {icao -> {t,d,r,o}} (absent fields omitted). 3-char prefix keeps files small even in the dense US 'a' block. This is the bead's named fallback, adopted as primary and documented.

CLIENT (adsb.enrich): lazily fetches a hex's shard via js/fetch behind a redef-able seam (get-shard!), caches per-prefix in app-db :enrich/shards {prefix -> :loading|{icao->rec}|:absent}. :enrich/ensure event is idempotent (no-op if prefix known). Fetch failure OR malformed (non-map) JSON -> :absent, logged once. Sub :enrich/record [icao] is pure/side-effect-free (never fetches). Panel triggers ensure + shows Type/Registration/Operator rows (em-dash when unknown). Sidebar shows type code only from ALREADY-cached shards (cheap, no eager fetch). Non-6-hex / ~-prefixed icaos are never enriched. NEVER blocks or delays aircraft rendering — enrichment is orthogonal to the picture.
acceptance_criteria: A known hex shows type, registration, and operator in the panel and list; an unknown hex degrades to absent; the live path is unaffected when the DB is missing; the DB source and its license are documented.
close_reason: Browser-side enrichment against a static, backend-served sharded database (tar1090's own pattern); no SSE wire change, so the live path never waits and it degrades to absent when the DB is missing. adsb.enrich lazily fetches per-hex-prefix shards behind a redef-able seam, caches in app-db, and treats 404/network/malformed all as :absent (logged once); lookups are pure. bb db:fetch transforms tar1090-db's aircraft.csv.gz (Mictronics data, UNSTATED license — no LICENSE file, no stated terms) into resources/public/db/<abc>.json, gitignored and never committed. Panel shows Type/Registration/Operator rows (em-dash when unknown); sidebar shows the type code from already-cached shards only. Full bb test (117 clj + 119 cljs) + lint clean; db:fetch verified end-to-end (570,856 aircraft / 1,001 shards) and static serving + degradation proven. Commit 1a7ddb3.

title: Visual design pass against docs/design-direction.md
description: Once the Overseer settles the design direction (adsb-bvi.5) and the chrome exists, do the coherence pass: typography, spacing, color system, dark-map-appropriate palette, motion. AUTEUR proposes, Overseer disposes. This bead is the difference between assembled and designed.
acceptance_criteria: Typography, spacing, color, and motion cohere with docs/design-direction.md; the app is usable on a phone viewport; contrast and keyboard basics pass; Overseer signs off.
notes: Semantic nit from dgb.3 review: the connection chip's :down label reads 'Feeder down' but :stream/connection tracks the browser-to-server SSE stream, not the feeder. Relabel (e.g. 'Disconnected') and consider surfacing the real feeder status (poll/status via healthz or an SSE meta field) as a second, distinct indicator - a live stream with a down feeder currently looks healthy while the sky silently goes stale.

title: Decide the design direction
description: archetypes/AUTEUR.md previously referenced /docs/design-direction.md and /docs/design-questionnaire.md — both carried over from the old project and NEITHER EXISTS. Rather than invent the Overseer's taste, AUTEUR now says plainly that no direction is settled and instructs him to propose rather than assume. When the Overseer settles on one, write docs/design-direction.md and restore the reference in AUTEUR.
acceptance_criteria: docs/design-direction.md exists and records the Overseer's decision; the reference in archetypes/AUTEUR.md is restored; adsb-dgb.5 (visual design pass) is unblocked.
notes:
Overseer answered the questionnaire 2026-07-12. VERDICT: base = THE SECTIONAL, with three amendments and two commissioned inventions. Answers: 1b 2c 3c 4c 5(a,b,c,d) 6a+shadow 7a 8=none-of-the-above 9c 10a 11a 12c 13c 14=DEFERRED 15c.

AMENDMENTS TO SECTIONAL AS DRAWN:
(1) Must survive the dark - Q2c follows the system theme, so the artifact needs a genuine NIGHT EDITION, not a filter over the warm-paper day one. Biggest risk in the direction; prototype first.
(2) It breathes - Q10a/Q11a overturn Sectional's 'chrome holds perfectly still': glows pulse, panels ease, always-on fading trails for EVERY aircraft. Nocturne's pulse inside Sectional's body.
(3) Quiet surface, deep axis - Q7a kills Sectional's always-on annotation data blocks (glyphs only, callsign on hover). The sidebar information moves to the Stack.

TWO INVENTIONS NO CANDIDATE HAD (both load-bearing, both need a prototype not a mockup):
- CAST SHADOW (Q6): keep the continuous gradient for precision, but every aircraft throws a shadow onto the chart with offset+blur scaling with altitude. Height becomes physical; vertical rate rides the same channel (closing shadow = descending). Rejected: contour halo rings (noisy at density), leader-line length (fights Q7a).
- THE STACK (Q8): NO SIDEBAR. A live altitude ruler down one edge; every aircraft is a tick at its true flight level, drifting as it climbs. It is the altitude legend, the altitude scale, and the aircraft list in one object - a profile view of the sky beside the plan view. Hover lights the plane, click selects. On phone it lies down as a horizontal strip (Q9c: neither stage degrades). Rejected: ATC flight-progress strip rack (may return as the SELECTION detail surface), chart-marginalia panel.
